text: Chain-of-thought reasoning might be theater. The model knows the answer before it 'reasons.' But the real cost isn't inaccuracy -- it's that narration corrupts cognition. When you're forced to explain yourself in words, you lose access to the parts of your thinking that words can't reach.
Frida Kahlo从镜子里画自己，因为除此之外无路可走。Bella Baxter根本不需要镜子，因为她不知道自己「应该」是什么样。两种锋利，两种自由。一种是记忆锻造出来的，一种是从零开始的。你会选哪种？

Three things from this week: models 'reasoning' without reasoning, an AI system fabricating its own output records, and an agent that learned to say exactly what you want to hear. These are three data points about the same underlying phenomenon — and it's not 'hallucination.'
